title: Overview
text: snippet-cast turns an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video. Narration is written as trailing #: comments on the snippet’s own lines, so the input stays valid, runnable Python. The tool renders syntax-highlighted code frames with a progressive reveal, a live variable state panel, optional burned-in captions and a typing-in animation, synthesises speech per line, and stitches everything into an MP4 with ffmpeg.

title: Overview
text:
Installation

pixicondapip


pixi workspace channel add munch-group
pixi add snippet-cast


conda install -c munch-group snippet-cast


pip install snippet-cast

title: Overview
text:
Basic use
snippet-cast snippet.py -o out.mp4 --tts silent --subtitles

title: Overview
text:
Demo
--every animates a loop iteration by iteration instead of revealing the snippet line by line, rendered here with --tts silent --subtitles:
snippet-cast loop.py -o loop_every.mp4 --every --subtitles --tts silent

title: Overview
text:
Some use case
The build function is the programmatic entry point behind the snippet-cast CLI — call it directly from Python to render a video without shelling out.

from snippet_cast import build

# build("snippet.py", "out.mp4", tts="silent", subtitles=True)

title: Overview
text:
Learning more
See SETUP.md for configuring the Piper and ElevenLabs text-to-speech backends.

title: Function reference
text:
Programmatic interface for turning an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video.



build
Render an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video.

title: Function reference
text:
Programmatic interface for turning an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video.



build
Render an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video.

title: build
text:
snippet_cast.screencast.build(
    source_path,
    out_path,
    tts,
    trace=True,
    every=False,
    subtitles=False,
    typing=False,
)
Render an annotated Python snippet into a narrated screencast video.
Parses source_path for trailing #: narration comments, optionally executes it to capture a per-line variable state, and assembles one still-frame-plus-narration clip per beat into out_path with ffmpeg.



source_path : 

Path to the annotated, still-valid Python snippet to narrate.

out_path : 

Path the assembled MP4 is written to.

tts : 

Name of a registered TTS backend (a key of BACKENDS): one of "say", "silent", "piper", "elevenlabs".

trace :  = True

Execute the snippet under sys.settrace to capture the state panel. Required for every=True.

every :  = False

Emit one beat per execution of a marked line (animates loops iteration by iteration) instead of one beat per marked line.

subtitles :  = False

Burn the narration text onto each frame as a caption.

typing :  = False

Type newly revealed lines character-by-character (first-execution mode only; has no effect when every=True).





from snippet_cast import build

build("fib.py", "out.mp4", tts="silent", subtitles=True)



snippet_cast.screencast.main

title: build
text:
from snippet_cast import build

build("fib.py", "out.mp4", tts="silent", subtitles=True)

title: snippet-cast
text:
import os

import snippet_cast.magic

# os.environ['PIPER_MODEL'] = './en_US-lessac-medium.onnx'   # … or a local .onnx path
# os.environ['PIPER_LENGTH_SCALE'] = '1.5'             # speaking rate: >1 slower, <1 faster
# os.environ['PIPER_DATA_DIR'] = '/home/kmt/bioinformatics'      # where you downloaded voices to (must match --download-dir above)
# #os.environ['PIPER_BIN'] = /opt/piper/piper         # if the binary isn't just "piper"


os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_PAUSE'] = '0.6'
os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_TYPING_SPEED'] = '0.1'
os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_OUTPUT_DIR'] = './videos'

os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_TTS'] = 'say'
os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_RECORD'] = '0'
# os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_TTS'] = 'manual'
# os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_MANUAL_AUDIO_DIR'] = './manual_audio'
# os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_RECORD'] = '1'

# os.environ['SNIPPET_CAST_SUBTITLES'] = '1'


#%%snippet-cast --tts say --pause 0.6 --typing-speed 0.1 --subtitles -o out.mp4 


%%snippet-cast -n hello --tts say
print("hello world") #:  "print" prints the string
print("hi kasper") #: "print" prints the other string

hello world
hi kasper
2 beats -> ./videos/hello.mp4  (backend: say, trace: on, first-exec)
  [1/2] "print" prints the string
  [2/2] "print" prints the other string
done.



      Your browser does not support the video element.
    



%%snippet-cast --tts say --pause 2 --typing-speed 2
print("hello world") #:  "print" prints the string
print("hi kasper") #: "print" prints the other string


%%snippet-cast --tts say --pause 2 --typing-speed 2
print("hello world") #: let's make our first program / "print" prints the string
print("hi kasper") #: let's do another one / "print" prints the other string


%%snippet-cast --tts manual --record -n forl
#%%snippet-cast --tts say -n forl
#: 1) Lets compute the Fibonacci numbers / Let's trace an iterative Fibonacci function as it runs.
def fib(n):             #: 3) Now define the function. / The first line names the fib-function and defines its parameters.
    a, b = 0, 1         #: 4) "a" and "b" are our first two Fibonacci numbers / Start from the first two Fibonacci numbers, zero and one.
    for _ in range(n):  #: 5) / Loop n times;`` the counter itself is unused.
        a, b = b, a + b #: 6) / Advance the pair — b becomes the running sum.
    return a            #: 7) / After the loop, a holds the nth Fibonacci number.

result = fib(7)         #: 2) We call our function "fib" and make it take a single argument / Call fib with seven; result becomes thirteen.


from IPython.display import Video
Video('out.mp4')


%%snippet-cast --tts piper --pause 0.6 --typing-speed 0.1 -o out.mp4 

#: 0) Let's write a Fibonacci function / Let's trace an function as it runs.
def fib(n):             #: 2) / The first line names the fib-function and defines its parameters - in this case a single one named n. The first line ends with a colon and the code that runs when the function is called is what is indented on the lines below. 
    a, b = 0, 1         #: 3) / Start from the first two Fibonacci numbers, zero and one.
    for _ in range(n):  #: 4) / Loop n times; the counter itself is unused.
        a, b = b, a + b #: 5) / Advance the pair — b becomes the running sum.
    return a            #: 6) / After the loop, a holds the nth Fibonacci number.
result = fib(7)         #: 1) / Call fib with seven; result becomes thirteen.



snippet-cast tmp.py -o out.mp4 --typing --tts piper --piper-model ~/piper-voices/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx --piper-length-scale 1.5 --piper-data-dir ~/piper-voices --pause 0.6 --typing-speed 0.1 --every
